member.name: T:GearGoblin.Core.JobPriorities
member.summary: Per-job materia stat-priority tables. Look up via using a job's three-letter abbreviation (PLD, WAR, GNB, WHM, ...). Falls back to a role-derived default for unrecognized abbreviations (e.g. a future job we haven't tabled yet).

member.name: F:GearGoblin.Core.JobPriorities.JobTable
member.summary: Job abbreviation → priority list. Twenty-one combat jobs (Endwalker roster + Dawntrail additions VPR + PCT). Each list is ordered from highest priority to lowest; the optimizer rotates through it once per empty materia slot. Stat names use FFXIV in-game BaseParam display strings (with "Direct Hit Rate" rather than "Direct Hit") so callers don't need to re-canonicalize.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.JobPriorities.For(System.String)
member.summary: Resolve the stat-priority list for the given job abbreviation. Returns a defensive copy so callers can iterate without holding a reference to the internal table. If the job isn't tabled (e.g. a new job from a future patch we haven't seen yet), falls back to the GNB tank priority — the same fallback the v0.6.0/0.6.1/0.6.2 web shipped, so unrecognised jobs keep working the way they used to instead of erroring.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.JobPriorities.IsTableHit(System.String)
member.summary: Whether the given job has a real entry in the priority table. False means callers got the fallback (GNB tank baseline) from . UI surfaces should display a clear "fallback" indicator so users know recommendations aren't job-tuned.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.JobPriorities.SourceLabel(System.String)
member.summary: Human-readable description of the priority source. Surfaced in the web's Materia Advisor badge ("AST priority", "GNB FALLBACK (no table for XYZ)", etc.) and in the plugin's About tab.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.JobPriorities.AllTabledJobs
member.summary: All job abbreviations with table entries. Useful for diagnostics and for the Settings tab when it eventually lets users browse and override priorities.

member.name: T:GearGoblin.Core.MateriaTiers
member.summary: Materia grade → stat value lookup. Grades are 1-indexed (Grade 1 = Tier I, Grade 12 = Tier XII). Stat values shown here are for substat materia (Crit, DH, Det, SkS, SpS, Ten, Pie) — the only materia kinds the optimizer recommends.

member.name: F:GearGoblin.Core.MateriaTiers.CurrentCapTier
member.summary: Current cap tier — the highest tier worth recommending in patch 7.x. Updated each major patch.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.MateriaTiers.SubstatValue(System.Int32)
member.summary: Substat stat-value at a given tier. Returns the Tier XII value for out-of-range tiers so callers don't crash on bad input.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.MateriaTiers.RomanNumeral(System.Int32)
member.summary: Convert a tier number to its roman-numeral name suffix, e.g. RomanNumeral(12) == "XII". Used by the optimizer to build the materia name string ("Savage Aim Materia XII").

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.MateriaTiers.NameOf(System.String,System.Int32)
member.summary: Build the in-game materia name for a stat at a tier, e.g. NameOf("Critical Hit", 12) == "Savage Aim Materia XII". Names match the in-game item display strings.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.MateriaTiers.MateriaPrefix(System.String)
member.summary:
Materia prefix per stat — the words that go in front of "Materia XII" to form the in-game item name. Casters and tanks share the same materia for any given substat (a Critical Hit materia is a Critical Hit materia regardless of who melds it), so this is a pure stat-to-prefix lookup.
v0.6.4 — Skill Speed prefix corrected from "Piety" to "Quickarm" (the actual in-game item name). The v0.6.3 stub used "Piety" as a placeholder during overnight Core ship; this is the proper value. Piety has its own materia ("Piety") and Skill Speed has "Quickarm"; the two were transposed.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.MateriaTiers.AllTiers
member.summary: All tiers we have stat values for. Iteration order is 1 → 12.

member.name: T:GearGoblin.Core.StatNames
member.summary: Canonicalize FFXIV substat names to a three-letter key. Accepts the abbreviated form ("CRT", "DH", "DET", "SKS", "SPS", "TEN", "PIE"), the in-game BaseParam display name ("Critical Hit", "Direct Hit Rate", "Determination", "Skill Speed", "Spell Speed", "Tenacity", "Piety"), and common variants (case-insensitive, whitespace-stripped, with or without "Rate" suffix on Direct Hit).

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.StatNames.Canonical(System.String)
member.summary:
Convert any plugin-emitted or hand-typed substat name into its canonical three-letter form. Returns an empty string for null/whitespace input and the uppercased input verbatim for anything we don't recognise — callers can then decide whether an unrecognised stat name is a parse error or just an unfamiliar future addition.
Examples:
Canonical("Critical Hit") → "CRT"
Canonical("crt") → "CRT"
Canonical("Direct Hit Rate") → "DH"
Canonical("DirectHit") → "DH"
Canonical("Spell Speed") → "SPS"
Canonical(null) → ""

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.StatNames.DisplayName(System.String)
member.summary: Reverse of — go from canonical key to the in-game BaseParam display name, which is what gets written into ExportMateriaV1.StatName on the wire and into the in-game character panel rows.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.StatNames.IsKnown(System.String)
member.summary: Whether is one of the seven known substat keys. Useful for filtering wire payloads.

member.name: M:GearGoblin.Core.StatNames.IsSpeed(System.String)
member.summary: Is this stat a "Speed" stat (Skill Speed or Spell Speed)? Used by the role-aware Stat Profile grid to pick which one to show in the speed slot.
